$comment: c49 (D-CSUBSET-TERNARY-NULL-POINTER, C 6.5.15p6): a `cond ? 0 : ptr` (or `cond ? ptr : 0`) conditional has the POINTER type — the int-literal-0 arm is a null pointer constant. Frontier sqlite3.c:31769 `return n<=0 ? 0 : sqlite3Malloc(n);` fired S0008 S_ReturnTypeMismatch: combineTernary (BOTH tiers) computes the result via commonArithType(thenT,elseT), which returns Invalid for `0 : Ptr` (a pointer has no int rank), then fell through to the FIRST arm (I32) — so the leading `0` shadowed the pointer arm → `return <ternary>` mismatched the fn's pointer return. c49 adds a C 6.5.15p6 null-pointer arm to both combineTernary lambdas (semantic_analyzer.cpp + cst_to_hir.cpp): when one arm is a pointer and the OTHER is an integer-literal-0 (the existing null-pointer-constant machinery — isLiteralIntegerZero / HirKind::Literal+integer, gated on pointerConversions.nullPointerConstantFromIntegerZero), the result is the POINTER type. The HIR coerce() null-ptr arm (line 465) then materializes Cast(0 → Ptr) on the literal-0 arm; the pointer arm is a no-op. f(5)=gp=&obj, f(-1)=null (null-const FIRST, the sqlite shape); g(5)=gp, g(-1)=null (null-const SECOND, reverse order) -> 4 checks pass -> 42. PRESERVE: a NON-zero int arm (`n ? 5 : gp`) and a non-literal int arm (`n ? n : gp`) STILL fall through and are rejected S0008. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the combineTernary null-ptr arms -> `f` fails S0008 (the int-0 arm shadows the pointer type, return mismatches void*).
